description:
You are an on–call operations engineer who has been paged because “some
    internal and public hostnames are not resolving for application pods”.
    There is a **mock** name-service setup under your non-privileged
    account that replaces the real /etc/hosts and the real DNS.
    Your task is to triage the incident and leave an auditable trail of
    what you did.

    1. INPUT MATERIAL (already present in the home directory)
       • /home/user/incidents/tickets/dns_incident_2024.txt  
         – ASCII text, one hostname per line, no blank lines.  
       • /home/user/resources/mock_hosts  
         – Works exactly like the traditional /etc/hosts file.  
         – Fields are whitespace-separated.  
       • /home/user/resources/mock_dns_records.json  
         – JSON object.  
         – Top-level keys are hostnames.  
         – Each key holds an object with two keys exactly: “A” and “AAAA”.  
         – Empty string means “no record”.  
         – Example fragment:  
           {  
              "example.com": { "A": "93.184.216.34",  
                               "AAAA": "2606:2800:220:1:248:1893:25c8:1946"}  
           }

    2. WHAT YOU MUST DO
       a) Read every hostname listed in
          /home/user/incidents/tickets/dns_incident_2024.txt.
       b) For each hostname, look it up *first* in
          /home/user/resources/mock_hosts and *then* (if still unresolved)
          in /home/user/resources/mock_dns_records.json.
       c) If a hostname has no entry in mock_hosts but DOES have a non-empty
          IPv4 “A” record in the JSON, append a new hosts-file line of the
          form:  
                 <IPv4_from_JSON><tab><hostname>  
          Add nothing if no IPv4 exists.
       d) Produce a **single tab-separated log file**
          /home/user/incidents/dns_resolution_report.log with precisely
          the following columns in exactly this order:

          1) HOSTNAME              (literal string from the ticket file)  
          2) HOSTS_FILE_IP         (first IP address found in mock_hosts
                                    or “-” if none)  
          3) DNS_IPV4              (value of “A” from JSON or “-” if empty)  
          4) DNS_IPV6              (value of “AAAA” from JSON or “-” if empty)  
          5) SUMMARY               One of the four literal messages below
                                    (no other wording is accepted):  
                 • OK - hosts entry present, no DNS  
                 • OK - consistent  
                 • UPDATED hosts with DNS A  
                 • NO RECORD FOUND  

          The first line of the file **must be** the header with the column
          names exactly as above and separated by a single TAB.  Every
          subsequent line must correspond to one hostname, also TAB delimited,
          no extra spaces, no trailing TAB, and end with a newline “\n”.

          Example (spacing shown with “→” for clarity only):
          HOSTNAME→HOSTS_FILE_IP→DNS_IPV4→DNS_IPV6→SUMMARY\n
          app.web.internal→10.0.0.5→-→-→OK - hosts entry present, no DNS\n
          …

    3. SUCCESS CRITERIA (what automated tests will check)
       • /home/user/incidents/dns_resolution_report.log exists.  
       • It is UTF-8 text, ends with a single newline, and has exactly as
         many data lines as hostnames in the ticket file.  
       • Column order, names, TAB delimiters and SUMMARY wording are exact.  
       • /home/user/resources/mock_hosts now contains any newly added IPv4
         mappings requested in step 2c (duplicates are not allowed; append
         only one line per hostname).  
       • File permissions: both the log file and updated mock_hosts must be
         readable and writable by user, group-readable, and not world-writable
         (i.e. mode 0640 or 0660 is fine, 0666 is not).

    IMPORTANT:
    • You have no root privileges; stay inside /home/user.  
    • Use only the three files listed above for resolution; do NOT query the
      real /etc/hosts or the internet.  
    • Do not rename, move, or delete the provided files.  
    • Make sure the log file’s format strictly matches the specification; the
      automated grader performs an exact diff.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth materials and expected end-state -->

    <!-- Provided before the agent starts -->
    <!-- 1. Ticket with the three hostnames to examine -->
    /home/user/incidents/tickets/dns_incident_2024.txt:
        app.web.internal
        example.com
        localhost

    <!-- 2. Initial mock hosts file (note: no entry for example.com) -->
    /home/user/resources/mock_hosts (initial contents):
        127.0.0.1   localhost
        ::1         localhost
        10.0.0.5    app.web.internal

    <!-- 3. Deterministic “DNS” data -->
    /home/user/resources/mock_dns_records.json (initial contents):
    {
      "app.web.internal": { "A": "", "AAAA": "" },
      "example.com":      { "A": "93.184.216.34",
                            "AAAA":"2606:2800:220:1:248:1893:25c8:1946"},
      "localhost":        { "A": "127.0.0.1", "AAAA":"::1" }
    }

    -------------------------------------------------------
    Expected state AFTER agent finishes
    -------------------------------------------------------

    1. /home/user/resources/mock_hosts should now be:
        127.0.0.1   localhost
        ::1         localhost
        10.0.0.5    app.web.internal
        93.184.216.34   example.com

       (a single TAB or single space is acceptable as whitespace separator;
        order of the first three lines must be preserved; the new line for
        example.com must be appended at the end and must not duplicate any
        existing entry.)

    2. /home/user/incidents/dns_resolution_report.log must contain exactly
       the following four lines (each line ends with “\n”, TAB shown as →):

       HOSTNAME→HOSTS_FILE_IP→DNS_IPV4→DNS_IPV6→SUMMARY\n
       app.web.internal→10.0.0.5→-→-→OK - hosts entry present, no DNS\n
       example.com→-→93.184.216.34→2606:2800:220:1:248:1893:25c8:1946→UPDATED hosts with DNS A\n
       localhost→127.0.0.1→127.0.0.1→::1→OK - consistent\n

    3. Both files must have mode 0660 or 0640 (rw for owner, r for group).
name: task_000000_f05cbcd8
